message:
fix(atc): use remaining_micros=0 for structural cooldown suppression (max_reps==0)

When max_reps == 0 (HighRiskIntervention), suppression is structural,
not time-based. The previous computation
`active_cooldown_micros.saturating_sub(elapsed)` could produce a negative
i64 when the cooldown window was 0 and time had elapsed — semantically
wrong inside a Suppressed variant. Now uses remaining_micros=0 with a
comment explaining that callers should check max_in_window == 0 to
distinguish structural from time-based suppression.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
